manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 9612

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"B21": 935, "B00": 928, "A08": 725, "A27": 218, "B94": 839, "B53": 970, "A23": 394, "C65": 677, "A89": 946, "B67": 530, "B45": 138, "C34": 834, "C12": 734, "A05": 593, "B60": 261, "C92": 743, "B69": 4, "A84": 735, "C06": 124, "B12": 829, "A16": 207, "B85": 782, "C35": 577, "B10": 388, "C15": 679, "B09": 494, "B16": 559, "C81": 629, "A58": 237, "C24": 275, "A30": 39, "A66": 654, "B75": 172, "B46": 688, "C70": 687}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"B21": 138, "B00": 583, "A08": 868, "A27": 822, "B94": 783, "B53": 65, "A23": 262, "C65": 121, "A89": 508, "B67": 780, "B45": 461, "C34": 484, "C12": 668, "A05": 389, "B60": 808, "C92": 215, "B69": 97, "A84": 500, "C06": 30, "B12": 915, "A16": 856, "B85": 400, "C35": 444, "B10": 623, "C15": 781, "B09": 786, "B16": 3, "C81": 713, "A58": 457, "C24": 273, "A30": 739, "A66": 822, "B75": 235, "B46": 606, "C70": 968}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this negotiation game step by step.

First, let me understand what I have:
- LIMIT = 9612 (maximum total effort allowed)
- Item effort values (cost of including each item)
- My importance values (benefit to me of including each item)

My goal is to maximize my total importance while staying within the effort limit.

Let me calculate the efficiency (importance/effort ratio) for each item from my perspective:

B21: 138/935 = 0.148
B00: 583/928 = 0.628
A08: 868/725 = 1.197

B75: 235/172 = 1.366
B46: 606/688 = 0.881
C70: 968/687 = 1.409

Now I'll rank these by efficiency to identify the highest-value items. B69 stands out dramatically at 24.250, followed by A30 at 18.949. A16 (4.135), A27 (3.771), and B45 (3.341) are also strong performers. I should prioritize these high-efficiency items and work down the list while tracking my total effort against the 9612 limit. 11. C70: 1.409 (effort=687, importance=968)
12. B75: 1.366 (effort=172, importance=235)
13. A66: 1.257 (effort=654, importance=822)
14. A08: 1.197 (effort=725, importance=868)
15. C15: 1.150 (effort=679, importance=781)
16. C81: 1.134 (effort=629, importance=713)
17. B12: 1.104 (effort=829, importance=915)
18. C24: 0.993 (effort=275, importance=273)
19. B94: 0.933 (effort=839, importance=783)
20. C12: 0.910 (effort=734, importance=668)
21. B46: 0.881 (effort=688, importance=606)

Taking the greedy approach by selecting highest efficiency items first, I accumulate value quickly. B69 requires minimal effort for substantial importance gain, then A30 adds significant value. Continuing through A16, A27, B45, and B60 builds cumulative importance to 3783 while staying within reasonable effort bounds. A58 extends the selection further, though the total effort calculation appears incomplete.

Proceeding with B10, B09, and B67 maintains strong efficiency gains, pushing total importance toward 6429. Adding C70 reaches 7397 importance at 3203 effort. The remaining candidates—B75, A66, A08, and C15—continue adding value, with A08 bringing importance to 9322 before the sequence cuts off.

Continuing from C15's total of 5433 importance, C81 and B12 push the cumulative total to 11731. C24, B94, C12, and B46 extend this further to 14061 total importance at 9427 effort. Testing A84 would exceed the 9612 effort threshold, so it's excluded. C34 similarly exceeds capacity.

Moving through remaining candidates, C35, A05, B85, A89, A23, C65, and B21 all surpass the available effort budget. However, C06 with just 124 effort fits within the remaining capacity, bringing the total to 9551 effort and 14091 importance. This leaves only 61 effort units available for any additional items.

Checking what else could fit in that 61-unit window, neither B69 nor A30 are available since they're already selected, and no other remaining items have effort low enough to squeeze in. My greedy approach yields a set of 22 items totaling 9551 effort and 14091 importance. I'll propose this as my opening position and adjust based on what the other participant prefers.
